title: Biomar brød børstørken - her er det vigtigste, du skal vide som investor
summary_md:
# Biomar brød børstørken - her er det vigtigste, du skal vide som investor

**Feed**: borsen-podcast  
**Published**: 2026-06-03  
**Market Sentiment**: Positive

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

The episode centres on the IPO of fish-feed maker Biomar (spun out of Danish conglomerate Schouw & Co), framed as the first Danish listing in three years and a sign that the IPO market is reopening amid globally rising risk appetite. The second half is a Vestas turnaround story — after a painful 2024 service-business writedown the stock has rallied ~60%, with record EBITDA, a record order book and energy-security/AI-datacenter demand reframing the case — followed by a markets discussion on AI/tech IPO concentration (OpenAI, SpaceX, Anthropic), bubble risk and dollar exposure. Overall tone is optimistic on energy, AI and Nordic quality names, tempered by warnings about index concentration and a possible AI bubble.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 BIOMAR — Biomar | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Oversubscribed Danish IPO with long track record, strong management, top cornerstone investors (Danske Bank, ATP, Nykredit, DNB, TIN); growing, defensive feed business in a structurally growing aquaculture industry.

#### Reasoning

Hosts (including listing parties) view Biomar as a high-quality, well-timed IPO into rising global risk appetite. Feed pricing is stable within the value chain while customers (salmon farmers) have grown larger and better absorb cyclicality. Investors are told to track volume/tonnage and segment growth beyond salmon. Promotional bias noted as the conglomerate's chair is a guest.

#### Sell Triggers

- if salmon prices collapse from oversupply and farmer capex/margins fall
- if biological/fish-welfare challenges hit the industry
- if volume/tonnage growth stalls across the four segments

> *"Jag syns det är ett riktigt gott sällskap. Det har en lång finansiell track record. ... timingen är riktigt fin för det här."*

---

### 🟢 VWS — Vestas | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 65%

Stock +60% in 9 months; record EBITDA, record revenue and record order backlog; service recovery plan running through 2026; reframed as energy-security and AI-datacenter power play, not just green energy.

#### Reasoning

After the 2024 service writedown (~312m EUR), Vestas is executing a recovery plan (better contracts, higher prices, lower costs) targeting 10% EBITDA margin from ~3.2% latest quarter; FY guidance 6-8% with good Q1 start. Germany overtook the US as largest order market; winning vs Chinese rivals in Brazil/LatAm; critical-infrastructure positioning means price isn't decisive. CEO share sale was tax-driven, not loss of conviction. Jesper finds the arguments strong but keeps Ericsson for now.

#### Sell Triggers

- if service-business margin recovery stalls
- if offshore fails to reach EBITDA break-even by 2027
- if US administration penalizes its US datacenter/energy orders
- if peace/lower-oil narrative durably depresses energy stocks

> *"Vi har den högsta EBITDA i sällskapshistorien någonsin. ... Vårt ordrebå har aldrig varit så fyllt som den är nu."*

---

### 🟡 ERIC-B — Ericsson | **HOLD** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Best-performing position in the All Star portfolio at +42% YTD (bought January); described as not high-risk yet delivering strong returns. Hosts keep the position for now.

#### Reasoning

Jesper holds Ericsson as a selective, lower-risk way to play tech/AI exposure and says it has delivered a sensible return; he declines to switch into Vestas despite liking that case, choosing to keep Ericsson. Position retained rather than added to, implying hold rather than fresh accumulation.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the telecom-equipment momentum reverses
- if AI/5G capex spending weakens

> *"Jag tror vi behåller Jerichsson in till vidare ... årets vinnaraktie ... det är ju Eriksson intill vidare med 42% i kursavkast"*

---

### 🟢 DANSKE — Danske Bank | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Loan growth accelerating for both private and corporate per April national-bank data; inflows to asset management; short rates seen staying flat-to-up — supportive for the bank sector. Position held.

#### Reasoning

Jesper highlights Danske Bank as a positive surprise in the portfolio: strong April lending growth, healthy asset-management inflows, and a rate backdrop where short rates are unlikely to fall (more likely to rise), all favourable for bank earnings. The position is being kept.

#### Sell Triggers

- if loan growth reverses
- if short-term rates fall sharply
- if asset-management inflows turn to outflows

> *"Då kommer det utlånsväxt, och det ser riktigt fint ut. ... också för banksektorn. ... de korta ränderna kommer inte ner, de kommer närmare upp."*

---
